page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 architecture CPU is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance is provisioned with 120.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 3.75 GB per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in single-threaded and multi-threaded operations. The server achieves top-tier results in text processing and text generation for small 135M language models, reaching 411.9 tokens per second. Single-threaded gzip compression at 33.79 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 201.71 GB/sec also place in the top 25% of tested hardware. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks drops to 147.21 GB/sec for reads and 62.26 GB/sec for writes, which represents average performance.

While the c4-standard-32 excels at CPU-bound tasks like physics simulations, text processing, and small-to-medium model prompt processing, it exhibits a clear bottleneck when handling larger workloads. Specifically, text generation using a large 70B LLM model falls into the bottom 10% of benchmarks at 2.26 tokens per second, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. Consequently, this instance is highly suited for static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications, but is not recommended for large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) and 120.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, it relies entirely on its 2.3 GHz CPU and 260 MB L3 cache. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities in text processing, single-threaded compression, and small-model LLM text generation, where it achieves top-tier throughput. However, memory bandwidth and large-model LLM text generation are notable bottlenecks, with 70B model generation falling into the bottom 10% of results. This server is highly efficient for static web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale database operations, but is poorly suited for heavy deep learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 is a general-purpose server with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 120 GB RAM, delivering strong performance for web serving and text processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32 features 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 120 GB RAM, optimized for web serving and text processing workloads.
tagline: A dedicated Intel Xeon server balancing high-throughput text processing and web serving without local storage or GPU overhead.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8581C processor.
Equipped with 120 GB of RAM and a large 260 MB L3 cache.
Top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation.
Strong throughput for static web serving and data decompression.
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators.
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and enterprise applications.
